articles.headline: What United Airlines' New Relax Row Economy Beds Will Likely Cost On Long-Haul Flights
articles.blurb: These beds could come with an appealing price tag.
articles.takeaway: United's economy bed product positions premium economy as a lower-cost alternative to business class, potentially capturing price-sensitive long-haul passengers willing to pay $500-1,000 more than standard economy.

articles.headline: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch

Filled with approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo, the Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL...

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Aviation's shift from investigating accidents after they happen to preventing them beforehand represents a fundamental transformation in how the industry prioritizes safety culture and organizational accountability.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Still Use 3-3 Seating On Narrowbody Jets?
articles.blurb: Narrowbody aircraft are limited by regulations to max out at 3-3 seating, while changing their fundamental design means they are not narrowbodies.
articles.takeaway: Regulatory constraints on narrowbody fuselage width create a hard ceiling on single-deck capacity, forcing airlines to choose between density limits or investing in widebody aircraft redesigns.

articles.headline: A-10 Thunderbolt II Crashed Near the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Officials Tell NYT

U.S. officials told NYT that the jet’s pilot was safely rescued. While search and rescue efforts for at least one of the crew members of an […]...
articles.takeaway: The near-simultaneous loss of two distinct U.S. Air Force aircraft in the same region suggests potential coordinated threat activity or environmental conditions warranting immediate fleet-wide operational review.

articles.headline: The Striking Differences Between The F-35 & F-22
articles.blurb: The F-22 Raptor and F-35 Lightning II represent two contrasting philosophies in modern airpower: air superiority versus multirole versatility.
articles.takeaway: The F-22's air dominance focus versus F-35's multirole capability reflects strategic trade-offs between specialized performance and operational flexibility that shape modern defense procurement.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across three integrated domains—airlines, airports, and flight operations—offers a more practical near-term pathway to sustainability than waiting for new aircraft technologies alone.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers
articles.blurb: NASA’s first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—are pioneering a new era of mission operations by integrating advanced lunar science directly into Mission Control. Their work will shape how future Artemis crews observe, analyze, and deepen our understanding of...
articles.takeaway: Dedicated science officers embedded in Mission Control will enable real-time mission optimization for lunar exploration, establishing an operational model likely to enhance efficiency across future deep-space programs.

articles.headline: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
articles.blurb: Italy’s Argotec has officially opened its first U.S. satellite production facility, cementing a foothold near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to join other foreign space firms pursuing growing demand from American programs.
articles.takeaway: Foreign satellite manufacturers are establishing U.S. production hubs to capture government contracts and bypass supply chain vulnerabilities tied to international dependencies.

articles.headline: Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom

As one of NASA’s first seven astronauts, he became America’s second astronaut to fly in space when he launched aboard the Liberty Bell 7 spacecraft on July 21, 1961, just weeks after...
articles.takeaway: Grissom's pioneering Mercury missions established critical operational procedures that became foundational to all subsequent crewed spaceflight programs.

articles.headline: Test Pilots and the Road Back to the Moon

articles.headline: Hello, World

There are two auroras (top right and bottom left) and zodiacal light (bottom right) is visible as the Earth eclipses the Sun. This and...
articles.takeaway: Artemis II's translunar trajectory provides unprecedented imagery of Earth's atmospheric phenomena, validating Orion's optical systems for deep-space navigation and mission-critical observation capabilities.

articles.headline: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments

articles.headline: DARPA’s X-65 Active Flow Control Demonstrator is Taking Shape
